title: Agent Trail v0.1.0
description: Validates a single Agent Trail JSONL record: trail envelope, session header, or event entry. File layout rules such as envelope position and multi-session grouping are enforced by whole-file validation; per-event payload shapes are enforced via the events subschemas.

iso8601.description: Writer timestamp: UTC ISO-8601 with millisecond precision. Format-aware validators may use the date-time annotation; whole-file validation rule 6 remains authoritative for calendar validity.

id.description: Globally-unique identifier shape: canonical uppercase ULID (26 Crockford base32 chars), lowercase hyphenated UUID (36 chars), or lowercase unhyphenated UUID (32 hex chars). Header ids, event ids, and envelope ids share this shape so cross-segment reconciliation can dedup by exact string equality (spec §9.5).

ulid.description: Canonical uppercase ULID (Crockford base32, 26 chars, no I/L/O/U). Time-prefixed and lexicographically sortable.

sessionUid.description: Globally-unique source-session identifier shape: canonical uppercase ULID (26 Crockford base32 chars), lowercase hyphenated UUID (36 chars), or lowercase unhyphenated UUID (32 hex chars). Reconcilers group segments by exact string equality on session_uid (spec §9.5).

segment.description: Multi-segment marker. Absent or {seq:1} for a single-segment trail. Reconciler primitive for daemon resume and multi-file sessions (spec §9.5).

seq.description: Segment sequence number within a source session.
Segment sequence number within a source session.

prev_content_hash.description: Content hash of the previous segment, or null when unavailable.

sha256Hex.description: SHA-256 hash as lowercase hex (64 chars)

oneOf.description: Custom unregistered agent using the x-<vendor>/<name> extension grammar, e.g. x-example/myagent

id.description: Stable identifier for this task plan item.

status.description: Current status of this task plan item.

active_form.description: Active-progress phrasing for this task plan item.

item_id.description: Identifier of the task plan item affected by this delta.

to_content.description: Task plan item text after this delta.

to_status.description: Task plan item status after this delta.

item_id.description: Identifier of the task plan item affected by this delta.

from_content.description: Task plan item text before this delta.

from_status.description: Task plan item status before this delta.

item_id.description: Identifier of the task plan item affected by this delta.

from_status.description: Task plan item status before this delta.

to_status.description: Task plan item status after this delta.

item_id.description: Identifier of the task plan item affected by this delta.

from_content.description: Task plan item text before this delta.

to_content.description: Task plan item text after this delta.

revision.description: Version control revision at session capture, or null for an unborn head.

branch.description: Active branch name required when revision is null.

type.description: Version control system kind or vendor-namespaced extension.

revision.description: Version control revision at session capture, or null for an unborn head.

remote_url.description: Canonical remote URL for the working tree. Adapters MUST normalize before emission: strip embedded credentials, strip trailing .git for git URLs, and normalize SSH/HTTPS variants to a single canonical form (https://host/path).

branch.description: Active branch / bookmark / topic name the session is running on. For git, the short branch name (e.g., `feature/x`). Detached-HEAD sessions MAY omit this field.

head_commit.description: Commit hash at session start (lowercase hex, 7-64 chars). For git this is typically the same value as `revision`; the field exists as an explicit, version-control-neutral alias and survives across VCS migrations.

worktree.description: Worktree context for the captured session.
Worktree context when the session ran inside a working-tree clone or worktree (git worktree, jj workspace, etc.).

name.description: Worktree name reported by the source.

original_cwd.description: Working directory of the parent repository at the time the worktree was created.

original_branch.description: Branch the parent repository was on when the worktree was created.

original_head_commit.description: Commit hash the worktree was forked from.

sourceMetadata.description: Adapter-provided metadata about the source event.

agent.description: Source agent that produced the original event.

original_type.description: Original source event type before Agent Trail normalization.

schema_version.description: Source schema or format version when reported.

raw.description: Opaque source object preserved verbatim. If an object, may use envelope_ref to reference an earlier entry's inlined envelope.

envelope_ref.description: Entry ID containing the inlined source envelope referenced by this raw object.

synthesized.description: Whether the adapter synthesized this metadata rather than copying it from source input.

semanticMetadata.description: Semantic linking for cross-event references when explicit IDs are unreliable.

group_id.description: Source semantic group identifier used for related events.

call_id.description: Source semantic tool-call identifier used for pairing.

tool_kind.description: Canonical tool kind associated with this semantic link.

agentMessageUsage.description: Token usage for this source agent envelope. May appear on agent_message, agent_thinking, or tool_call when that entry is the first entry derived from the envelope. input_tokens/output_tokens are deltas for this envelope; *_cumulative variants are running totals through this point. total_tokens/total_tokens_cumulative are source-reported inclusive totals for exact total-token analytics. cache_read_tokens and cache_creation_tokens are independent billing categories. context_input_tokens captures source-reported prompt/context pressure for this request, cache-inclusive when the source exposes enough detail; context_window_tokens captures the model context-window size when exposed. When present, usage must include either input/output coverage or total-token coverage.

input_tokens.description: Input token count reported for this source envelope.

output_tokens.description: Output token count reported for this source envelope.

input_tokens_cumulative.description: Cumulative input token count through this source envelope.

output_tokens_cumulative.description: Cumulative output token count through this source envelope.

total_tokens.description: Total token count reported for this source envelope.

total_tokens_cumulative.description: Cumulative total token count through this source envelope.

cache_read_tokens.description: Tokens read from model cache for this source envelope.

cache_creation_tokens.description: Tokens written to model cache for this source envelope.

reasoning_tokens.description: Reasoning token count reported for this source envelope.

context_input_tokens.description: Source-reported context input token pressure for this request.

context_window_tokens.description: Model context window size reported by the source.

attachment.description: An image or file carried by a message or tool result, by reference. v0.1.0 uri schemes are references only (https:, local file:, content-addressed sha256:); inline data: payloads are deferred.

kind.description: Attachment kind carried by the message or tool result.

media_type.description: Media type reported for the attachment.

name.description: Display or source name for the attachment.

parseFidelity.description: At-a-glance session parse fidelity summary. When present, quarantined_count MUST equal the number of x-*/unknown_record system_event entries in the session group; termination_reason MUST match the final session_terminated reason when one exists.

quarantined_count.description: Number of quarantined source records emitted as x-*/unknown_record system_event entries in this session group.

termination_reason.description: Final abnormal session termination reason, when a session_terminated event is present.

trailEnvelope.description: Optional trail envelope record (line 1). File-level metadata; not part of the event graph. When present, MUST appear at line 1 and the first session header MUST follow on line 2. At most one per file. Multi-session files (spec §9.6) carry one envelope followed by N session groups in file order.

schema_version.description: Agent Trail schema version for the envelope record.

id.description: Globally unique identifier for this trail envelope.

ts.description: Writer timestamp for the trail envelope.

producer.description: Producer name and version that wrote the trail file.

content_hash.description: Content hash for the finalized trail file, or pending marker while open.

tags.description: User-visible tags associated with the trail file.

vcs.description: Version control context for the trail file.

trail_id.description: Identifier of the prior trail artifact.

content_hash.description: Content hash for the finalized trail file, or pending marker while open.

fork_from.description: Prior trail artifact this trail was forked from.

content_hash.description: Content hash for the finalized trail file, or pending marker while open.

redacted_from.description: Prior content hash this redacted artifact derives from.

sessions.description: Optional manifest of sessions contained in the file, one entry per session group in file order (spec §8.4, §9.6). Validator warns on length mismatch or per-entry drift vs actual file content.

id.description: Globally unique identifier for this trail envelope.

agent.description: Canonical agent name for the session manifest entry.

meta.description: Free-form vendor extensions. Recommended keys use the x-<vendor>/<name> extension grammar.

header.description: Session header. The first session header is required at line 1, or at line 2 when a trail envelope occupies line 1. Multi-session files (spec §9.6) carry additional session headers later in the file; each opens a new (header, events*) group. Not part of the event graph.

schema_version.description: Agent Trail schema version for the session header.

id.description: Globally unique identifier for this session header.

tags.description: User-visible tags associated with the session.

session_uid.description: Globally-unique source-session identifier. Stable across all segments of one source session (spec §9.5). Reconcilers group segments by session_uid. Optional in v0.1 single-segment trails; writers SHOULD emit it for forward-compat. Required (and enforced by the header allOf if/then) when segment.seq > 1. ULID is recommended (lexicographic tie-breaker); UUID accepted.

segment.description: Multi-segment marker for this session group.

content_hash.description: Content hash for the finalized session group, or pending marker while open.

ts.description: Writer timestamp for the session header.

stream.description: Live-capture marker. Present means writer is actively appending or last appended in streaming mode. Absent means non-streaming or unaware writer.

state.description: Current live-capture state for the session stream.

started_at.description: Writer timestamp when live capture started.

model_default.description: Default model configured for the agent at session start.

agent.description: Agent runtime that produced the session.

cwd.description: Working directory where the session ran.

vcs.description: Version control context for the session.

content_hash.description: Content hash for the finalized session group, or pending marker while open.

entry_id.description: Entry identifier in the parent session that spawned this session.

fork_from.description: Parent session artifact this session was forked from.

content_hash.description: Content hash for the finalized session group, or pending marker while open.

redacted_from.description: Prior content hash this redacted session derives from.

parse_fidelity.description: Parse fidelity summary for this session group.

agent.description: Source agent name reported for imported session input.

path.description: Source file path for imported session input.

format_version.description: Source format version for imported session input.

source.description: Source file metadata for imported session input.

meta.description: Free-form vendor extensions. Recommended keys use the x-<vendor>/<name> extension grammar (spec §8.3).

allOf.description: Spec §9.5: session_uid is required when segment.seq >= 2 so reconcilers can group continuation segments. Single-segment trails (seq absent or seq=1) keep session_uid optional in v0.1.

session_uid.description: Source-session identifier required for continuation segments.

id.description: Globally unique identifier for this event entry.

parent_id.description: Parent event ID in the session tree, or null for a root event.

ts.description: Writer timestamp for this event entry.

semantic.description: Semantic linking metadata for this event.

redaction_count.description: Number of redactor mutations applied to this event entry.

meta.description: Agent Trail metadata for this event.

origin.description: Authorship marker for user-role text. Absent means user-authored.

attachments.description: Attachments carried by the user message.

model.description: Model that produced this agent message.

usage.description: Token usage attached to this agent message.

attachments.description: Attachments carried by the agent message.

explanation.description: Optional explanation accompanying the task plan update.

items.description: Full task plan state after the update.

deltas.description: Incremental task plan changes represented by this update.

tool.description: Canonical kind of tool requested by the agent.

args.description: Arguments supplied to the requested tool.

usage.description: Token usage attached to this tool call when it is the first usage-capable derived entry.

truncated.description: Whether tool-call arguments were truncated before emission.

args_size.description: UTF-8 byte length of the original args object before truncation. Required when truncated is true.

overflow_ref.description: Content-addressed reference to full arguments when arguments are truncated.

path.description: Path of the file to read.

range.description: Optional inclusive line range requested from the file.

args.description: Arguments for a file read tool call.

path.description: Path of the file to write.

args.description: Arguments for a file write tool call.

path.description: Path of the file to edit.

diff.description: Patch or diff content describing the edit.

path.description: Path of the file to edit.

old.description: Original text expected in the target file.

new.description: Replacement text for the target file.

replace_all.description: Whether all matching occurrences are replaced.

args.description: Arguments for a file edit tool call.

path.description: Path of the file changed by this patch entry.

files.description: Files changed by this patch request.

atomic.description: Whether the patch should be applied atomically.

args.description: Arguments for an atomic multi-file patch tool call.

glob.description: Optional glob filter for listed paths.

args.description: Arguments for a file listing tool call.

glob.description: Optional glob filter for searched paths.

args.description: Arguments for a file search tool call.

command.description: Shell command requested by the agent.

cwd.description: Working directory for the shell command.

args.description: Arguments for a shell command tool call.

command_id.description: Source command identifier whose output is requested.

args.description: Arguments for polling shell command output.

input.description: Input text sent to the running command.

args.description: Arguments for sending input to a running shell command.

tool.description: MCP tool name invoked on the server.

args.description: Tool-specific argument object passed to the MCP tool.

headers.description: Request headers supplied to the MCP call.

args.description: Arguments for an MCP tool call.

method.description: HTTP method requested by the fetch.

headers.description: Request headers supplied to the fetch.

args.description: Arguments for a web fetch tool call.

query.description: Search query submitted to the web search tool.

args.description: Arguments for a web search tool call.

query.description: Search query used to discover tools.

limit.description: Maximum number of tool results requested.

args.description: Arguments for a tool discovery search.

diff.description: Patch content for the notebook edit.

args.description: Arguments for a notebook edit tool call.

task.description: Task prompt or instruction given to the subagent.

args.description: Arguments for invoking a subagent.

name.description: Tool name reported by the source agent.

args.description: Opaque arguments for the unclassified tool call.
Opaque arguments for the unclassified tool call.

for_id.description: Tool call ID this result answers.

ok.description: Whether the tool call completed successfully.

truncated.description: Whether tool output was truncated before emission.

output_size.description: UTF-8 byte length of the original output before truncation. Required when truncated is true.

overflow_ref.description: Content-addressed reference to full output when output is truncated.

error.description: Error detail when the tool result failed, or null when unavailable.

attachments.description: Attachments produced by the tool result.

meta.description: Structured per-toolkind outputs, keyed by the originating tool_call.tool. Optional; consumers fall back to payload.output when the relevant key is absent. Registered keys are writer-strict; unregistered/future toolkinds are opaque objects. Vendors extend a registered key via x-<vendor>/<name> pattern keys.

text.description: Text payload for a text MCP content block.

data.description: Encoded data for a binary MCP content block.

mime_type.description: Media type for the MCP content block data.

uri.description: Resource URI for an MCP resource content block.

content_blocks.description: Structured MCP content blocks returned by the tool.

is_error.description: Whether the MCP tool result represents an error.

mcp_call.description: Structured output for an MCP tool result.

range.description: Line range represented by the file read output.

total_lines.description: Total line count reported for the read file.

encoding.description: Text encoding reported for the read output.

truncated_at_line.description: Line where file output was truncated, or null when not line-truncated.

file_read.description: Structured output for a file read result.

stdout.description: Standard output captured from the shell command.

stderr.description: Standard error captured from the shell command.

exit_code.description: Process exit code, or null when unavailable.

signal.description: Termination signal, or null when unavailable.

shell_command.description: Structured output for a shell command result.

scope.description: Abort granularity. tool_call aborts reference a specific tool_call by for_id; turn aborts describe a broader turn-level stop when the source cannot identify one call.

for_id.description: Tool call ID affected by this abort.

reason.description: Why execution stopped before a normal tool_result.

blocked_by.description: Source component or policy that blocked the tool call.

for_id.description: Tool call ID affected by this abort.

question.description: Question text presented to the user.

multi_select.description: Whether multiple options may be selected.

is_secret.description: Whether the answer should be treated as secret input.

allow_other.description: Whether a free-form other answer is allowed.

options.description: Predefined answer options for the question.

for_id.description: User query ID this response answers.

selected.description: Selected option IDs or labels for this answer.

answers.description: Answers keyed by user query question ID.

scope.description: Summary scope covered by this event.

model.description: Model that produced the summary when reported.

kind.description: Lifecycle/hook signal category. Either one of the reserved cross-agent values, or a vendor-namespaced extension of the form `x-<vendor>/<name>`.

data.description: Structured data associated with the system event.

model.description: Model that produced the thinking block.

level.description: Source-defined thinking effort or visibility level.

usage.description: Token usage attached to this thinking block.

reason.description: Reason for the user interrupt when reported.

summary.description: Compaction summary that replaces earlier context.

replaced_message_ids.description: Agent Trail entry IDs folded or replaced by this compaction summary. Provenance-only; readers must not require same-file resolution.

from_id.description: Entry ID where a branch starts.

abandoned_branch_id.description: Entry ID of the abandoned branch being summarized.

model.description: Model that produced the branch summary when reported.

from_model.description: Model in use before the change when known.

to_model.description: Model in use after the change.

from_provider.description: Model provider before the change when known.

to_provider.description: Model provider after the change when known.

trigger.description: Trigger that caused the model change.

turn_id.description: Source turn identifier associated with the model change.

scope.description: Mode domain changed by this event.

from_mode.description: Mode value before the change when known.

trigger.description: Trigger that caused the mode change.

turn_id.description: Source turn identifier associated with the mode change.

data.description: Structured data associated with the mode change.

from_level.description: Thinking level before the change when known.

trigger.description: Trigger that caused the thinking level change.

turn_id.description: Source turn identifier associated with the thinking level change.

data.description: Structured data associated with the thinking level change.

open_call_ids.description: Tool-call IDs still open when the session ended abnormally.

final_message_id.description: Final message or header ID associated with session completion.

name.description: User-visible identifier of the invoked capability. Leading slash for slash/builtin/custom_prompt commands (`/clear`); bare name for skills (`webapp-testing`).

kind.description: What kind of capability was invoked.

via.description: How the invocation reached the agent. `auto_trigger` covers description-matched skill activation with no user action; adapters MAY synthesize it (set source.synthesized=true).

args.description: Structured arguments for the invoked capability.

expansion_text.description: Text inserted or expanded by the invoked capability.

result_action.description: What the runtime did with the invocation. Either one of the reserved values, a vendor-namespaced extension of the form `x-<vendor>/<name>`, or null.

scope.description: Capability domain changed by this event.

snapshot.description: Full capability snapshot after this change.

previous_value.description: Previous session metadata value when known.
Previous session metadata value when known.
Previous session metadata value when known.
Previous session metadata value when known.

metadata.description: Structured metadata for the added capability.
